text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: with the click trigger enabled (`openOnClick`), clicking an anchor element is supposed to open its tooltip. The library implements this by listening for click events at the document level. Because that delegated listener runs during the event's bubbling phase, if the anchor's own click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`, the click never bubbles up to the document, the library's delegated listener never runs, and the tooltip fails to open. The correct behavior is that clicking the anchor opens its tooltip even when the anchor stops propagation of the click event. Fix how/when the library registers its delegated click handling so that a propagation-stopping anchor still opens the tooltip. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: with the click trigger enabled (`openOnClick`), clicking an anchor element is supposed to open its tooltip. The library implements this by listening for click events at the document level. Because that delegated listener runs during the event's bubbling phase, if the anchor's own click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`, the click never bubbles up to the document, the library's delegated listener never runs, and the tooltip fails to open. The correct behavior is that clicking the anchor opens its tooltip even when the anchor stops propagation of the click event. Fix how/when the library registers its delegated click handling so that a propagation-stopping anchor still opens the tooltip. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

I’ll inspect the tooltip event-delegation implementation and its existing interaction tests, then make the smallest source-level change that preserves current behavior while allowing `stopPropagation()` anchors to trigger click-open tooltips. I’ll run the focused Jest test and the required production build afterward.

The delegated listeners are centralized in `event-delegation.ts`; they currently attach in the default bubbling phase. I’m checking the surrounding event semantics and repository state before editing, because enabling capture globally would fix the click case but could unintentionally alter hover/focus ordering.

The safest scoped fix is to keep delegation’s current behavior for other event types and register the shared `click` listener in the capture phase. That lets the library observe the anchor click before the anchor can stop propagation, while preserving the existing delegated handler API and cleanup pairing. I’ll apply that source change and add a focused regression test for `openOnClick` plus `stopPropagation()`.

input.cmd: apply_patch <<'PATCH' *** Begin Patch *** Update File: src/components/Tooltip/event-delegation.ts @@ const handlersByType = new Map>() + +// Click handlers need to run before an anchor can stop the event from bubbling. +const delegatedEventCapture = (eventType: string): boolean => eventType === 'click' @@ set = new Set() handlersByType.set(eventType, set) - document.addEventListener(eventType, dispatch) + document.addEventListener(eventType, dispatch, delegatedEventCapture(eventType)) @@ handlersByType.delete(eventType) - document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch) + document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch, delegatedEventCapture(eventType)) @@ handlersByType.forEach((_handlers, eventType) => { - document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch) + document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch, delegatedEventCapture(eventType)) }) *** Update File: src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js @@ test('opens on click when click is the configured trigger', async () => { @@ expect(tooltip).toHaveTextContent('Click Only Test') }) + + test('opens on click when the anchor stops propagation', async () => { + await renderAndFlush( + <> + event.stopPropagation()} + > + Click Me + + + , + ) + + fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Click Me')) + await flushMicrotasks() + + const tooltip = await waitForTooltip('stopped-click-test') + expect(tooltip).toHaveTextContent('Stopped Click Test') + }) test('stops showing after scroll and resize global close events', async () => { *** End Patch PATCH node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js --runInBand

The patch utility is absent here, so I’m applying the same narrow edits through the provided file-edit API: capture-phase registration for delegated `click`, matching cleanup, and a regression test that exercises an anchor-level propagation stop.

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The production build passes. The full suite has one unrelated failure in the pre-existing imperative numeric-zero test (`isOpen` remained false while the tooltip was visibly rendered and closing); all 175 other tests passed, including the new regression. I’m rerunning that test alone to determine whether it is order/timing-related, then I’ll do a final status/diff check.

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

Implemented the fix in `src/components/Tooltip/event-delegation.ts`:

- Delegated `click` events are now registered during the capture phase.
- Cleanup and test reset use the matching capture option.
- This allows tooltip opening even when an anchor’s click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`.

Added a regression test covering `openOnClick` with propagation stopped.

Verification:
- Focused interaction tests: **9 passed**
- Production build: **passed**
- Full Jest suite: **175 passed, 1 pre-existing unrelated failure** in the imperative numeric-zero test.
